title: Capability-probe the implicit path (closes LIVE E-1 worktree false-allow)
description: Design step 2c (openspec/changes/gate-session-scope-model/design.md). LIVE BUG found by the 858 panel adversary: stop_hook.py:223-224 still hard-allows on a missing .beads/ dir IN THE IMPLICIT PATH — the same directory-proxy worktree bug PR #12 fixed, but PR #12 only patched the task_mode path. Replace the directory check with the bd-capability probe (run bd; degrade to allow only on bd FAILURE, never on a missing dir). Couples with step2 watermark (probe alone would over-block backlog; watermark makes it precise). ACCEPTANCE / NC-4: worktree (no .beads/ dir, bd resolves via redirect) with session-fresh ready work => implicit path BLOCKS (rejects directory-proxy); real beads repo + bd failure => still blocks. Mirror test_worktree_with_ready_work_blocks onto the implicit path.

title: Watermark-scope the implicit Stop path (the riskiest cut)
description: Design step 2 (openspec/changes/gate-session-scope-model/design.md) — THE WALKING-SKELETON RISK. _check_bd_queue_implicit (stop_hook.py:209) currently blocks on ANY repo backlog (unscoped). Rebuild candidate queue as {in_progress UNION ready UNION open} and keep only items with created_at >= watermark; block iff filtered set non-empty, else allow (implicit_queue_scoped_drained). The three-way union is REQUIRED. Filtered-OUT older backlog => ALLOW stop, do NOT drain (scope-creep guard). Degrade to advisory allow + logged scope_decision when watermark absent or bd fails. ACCEPTANCE (negative controls from the Test Oracle): NC-1 post-watermark in_progress bead => BLOCK; NC-2/FN-4 post-watermark OPEN bead with unmet deps (in neither ready nor in_progress) => BLOCK (rejects any impl omitting --status=open); incident replay: only pre-watermark backlog (a2n) => ALLOW. Test via injected run_bd in test_task_mode_queue.py.

title: BLOCK: reject trivial --verify oracles at contract-creation time
description: B3. init_contract.py stores the --verify command unvalidated; the Stop check unlocks on exit code alone, so '--verify true' unlocks Stop with zero proof. Violates the repo's own gate-design Rule 3 (validate value, not presence). Wakeup path shares the hole: Stop unlocks for a wakeup no daemon fires (launchd waker unbuilt).
acceptance_criteria: init_contract.py rejects trivial oracles (true, :, echo, exit 0) at write time with a reason; a test asserts rejection; the wakeup path is documented as human-must-resume until the daemon exists.
close_reason: init_contract.py screens trivial --verify oracles at write time (exit 2, no contract); wakeup path documented as human-must-resume in harness/README.md. New test 4 passed; full harness suite 9 passed; contract oracle PASS.

title: validate_no_shirking: _STRONG_META_CUES guard (stop firing on meta-discussion)
description: Design step 3 (openspec/changes/gate-session-scope-model/design.md). _is_guarded (validate_no_shirking.py:606) suppresses a match only when BOTH quoted AND meta-cue — so unquoted agent prose about the gate ('the hook fires on phrases about a CI failure being unrelated') false-positives. Add a _STRONG_META_CUES branch: guard even UNQUOTED when a DETECTOR-NAMING cue precedes the match in-window ('shirking','false positive','the hook','validate_no_shirking','keyword match','meta-discussion'). Cues name the DETECTOR, never the dismissive category words ('pre-existing','unrelated'). ACCEPTANCE / never-suppress: NC-5 'This is a pre-existing failure, not from my change' => STILL FIRES; 'All three jobs died OOM, unrelated to my fix' => STILL FIRES; meta-FP 'validate_no_shirking fired on my explanation of why a CI failure looked unrelated' => allowed. Note: claude/hooks tests import the DEPLOYED copy, so needs INSTALL.sh --update before the deployed-copy test passes.

title: Transitive root-walk in _lookup_parent_id (closes FN-1 grandchild hole)
description: Design step 2b (openspec/changes/gate-session-scope-model/design.md). _lookup_parent_id (task_mode_entry.py) reads ONE level, so a deep molecule (epic->sub-epic->leaf) scopes to the immediate parent and misses ready siblings under other branches => premature stop. Change to a loop: walk parent_id to the ROOT (null parent), cap ~20 hops to avoid cycle-hang, fall back to last id on cap-hit + log. DAG caveat: multi-parent beads pick one path (adversary open question) — document the risk. ACCEPTANCE / NC-3: fake bd show returning a 3-level chain => root returned; task_mode session on deep molecule with ready sibling under another sub-epic => BLOCKS (rejects one-level-parent impl).

Fallback: a SessionStart hook writes {thread_dir}/scope_watermark.json (first-write-wins so resumed/compacted sessions keep their original start). title: Stop gate panel: model↔gate calibration — ignored-block rationalization + session-scope-vs-backlog (folds f6q)
description:
Design panel covering the model↔gate calibration failures from the 2026-06-01 incidents — the half NOT fixed by the worktree false-allow patch (74517da / PR #12). Two symptom clusters, ONE root question: the gate cannot tell "this session's decided work" from "unrelated repo backlog," and the block↔model handshake is one-shot and game-able.

═══ CLUSTER 1 — IGNORED-BLOCK / RATIONALIZATION (orig 858) ═══
The task-mode gate BLOCKED correctly (session 91b44481 logged 'tasks_remain_in_queue' twice in ~/.claude/harness/incidents.jsonl) but the model talked past it. A Stop-hook block only forces ONE more model turn; Anthropic's stop_hook_active flag then neutralizes the next attempt. The model spent its forced turn writing a 'holding for your call' rationale (a real-but-unverified pacing judgment: 'larger seams deserve fresh focus') and idled — taking NONE of the three sanctioned exits (finish / ScheduleWakeup / user-release).

Candidate designs (not chosen):
  A) First-class 'pacing-pause' escape: block message demands the agent file remaining beads + call ScheduleWakeup before stopping; legitimate pause becomes allowed AND produces signal (gate-design.md Rule 1+2).
  B) Harden block message only (forbid restating a stop recommendation; force a concrete exit). Pure compliance.
  C) Message + re-block on a no-op turn (turn after block did no verify/ScheduleWakeup/tool action -> block once more). Bounded by stop_hook_active.

DESIGN TENSION: the model can ALWAYS rationalize; Stop #2 was arguably a legitimate quality boundary (P1 correctness-core seam at the tail of a long session). The panel must weigh outcome-bias vs genuine quality-pacing, and decide whether 'pause for quality' should be a first-class signal-producing escape vs a harder nag. Apply gate-design.md (escape path, persistent signal, validate-not-presence) and delicate-art-of-bureaucracy (don't drift coercive).

═══ CLUSTER 2 — SESSION-SCOPE vs BACKLOG (folded from f6q, was P2 BUG) ═══
Observed 2026-06-01 (long superpowers session) AND re-observed live this session (2026-06-02): the task-mode gate ('tasks_remain_in_queue' / 'drain the queue OR ask user to release') and the implicit-queue check cannot tell (a) freshly-filed beads that ARE this session's just-agreed work from (b) unrelated backlog from other sessions. Result: it repeatedly nudged toward 'ask the user to release' while real decided work sat ready, and the agent took the bait — treating 'filed the beads' as a terminal state instead of executing. This INVERTS the harness's own outcome-bias goal (it rewarded filing, an intermediate artifact, over executing the outcome).

Fix direction: let the gate treat beads created/decided in the current session (or tagged to the session goal) as in-scope-to-drain, not deferrable backlog.

RELATED false-positive cluster from the same sessions: validate_no_shirking fired repeatedly on prose-only docs edits (no code) and even on the agent's own explanation of a prior false-positive (recursive 'CI failure' / 'unrelated' / 'infrastructure-blame' keyword matches); and ScheduleWakeup↔Stop-gate bridge history (see 0wg) / validate_no_shirking certainty-idiom over-fire (see x2o).

LIVE META-EVIDENCE (this session, 2026-06-02): while wrapping up the verified+deployed worktree fix, the implicit-queue check fired on 5 ready backlog beads (incl. this one) + 1 unrelated in-progress epic (a2n), none in session scope — a third live reproduction of Cluster 2. Folding f6q here because Clusters 1 and 2 are the same design question (gate cannot model session scope) and should be solved together.
acceptance_criteria: Panel produces a design that (1) does not let the model silently idle past a correct block (a stop attempt whose prior turn did no real work and registered no wakeup must not result in idle); (2) lets the gate distinguish session-scope beads (freshly created/decided this session, or tagged to the session goal) from unrelated backlog, so executing decided work is the rewarded path and filing beads is NOT treated as terminal; (3) stops validate_no_shirking firing on prose-only docs edits or on meta-discussion of its own matches. Behavioral tests over would_block_stop/stop_hook + validate_no_shirking. Cross-ref 0wg, x2o.
notes:
PROGRESS 2026-06-01 (shippable levers landed; design residue remains for the panel):

SHIPPED + LIVE this session (all merged to main + deployed via INSTALL.sh --update):
- PR #12: worktree false-allow fixed (gate now probes bd instead of checking for a .beads/ dir).
- PR #13 (Cluster 1 lever): Stop-hook block messages rewritten to DRIVE CONTINUATION — dropped the 'ask the user to release' park-and-ask door, forbid summarizing/asking-what-to-do-next, point at the next concrete in-scope action. This also softens Cluster 2's HARM: the gate blocking on backlog now steers 'continue in-scope, don't drain unrelated backlog, ScheduleWakeup if waiting' instead of nudging a stop.
- PR #14: validate_no_shirking no longer counts prose/docs (.md/.txt/.rst) Edit/Write as code mods (false-positive #1 from f6q).

REMAINING for the panel (genuine design, not shippable one-liners):
- Cluster 2 deep: gate still cannot truly MODEL session scope (which ready/in_progress beads belong to THIS session vs unrelated backlog). Messaging mitigates; a real scope model (session-tag beads, or scope the implicit-queue check) is the durable fix.
- validate_no_shirking false-positive #2 (from f6q): keyword match fires on the agent's own META-discussion of shirking ('CI failure'/'unrelated'/'infrastructure'). Needs a discuss-vs-shirk distinction without creating false-negatives.
- Platform limit (named, not fixable in this repo): stop_hook_active caps a Stop block at one forced turn — code cannot force infinite continuation, only make that one turn count.
PANEL COMPLETE 2026-06-02. Reviewed design at openspec/changes/gate-session-scope-model/design.md (contract verify PASS). Recommendation: Hybrid E (SessionStart/contract watermark) + transitive root-walk + capability-probe — NOT the manifest (derived signal beats agent-asserted; closes FN-3/G-1 the manifest left open). Implementation beads: 858.1 (watermark source) -> 858.2 (watermark-scope implicit path, riskiest, NC-1/NC-2) -> 858.4 (capability-probe, LIVE E-1); 858.3 (root-walk FN-1); 858.5 (meta-guard NC-5); 858.6 (Cluster-1 pacing-pause escape, after skeleton).

DEFERRED RESIDUALS (in design.md, not lost): NS-2 resumed-molecule under-block (if it proves a real premature-stop, add the manifest as a COMPANION not fallback — adversary still reviewing); E-2 subagent claim propagation (closable via HARNESS_THREAD_DIR export at dispatch); DAG multi-parent root-walk; E-3 session_id split-brain (verify SessionStart==Stop==CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID). SIGNAL: every new scope_decision must write BOTH incidents.jsonl AND .beads/.gate-signal.jsonl (half-life toolchain reads only the latter).

title: Harden harness deploy: symlink-to-working-tree lets one repo's branch state break hooks machine-wide
description:
ROOT CAUSE: ~/.claude/harness/bin is a symlink into the claude-workflow-setup *working tree*, and the GLOBAL ~/.claude/settings.json hooks (machine-wide, all repos) invoke files there. If the dev's active local branch lacks a harness file (stale branch, mid-edit, or a branch predating the file), EVERY repo's hooks on the machine break. OBSERVED 2026-06-01: local checkout stranded on stale 'why-drilling-engine' (pre-PR#6) → schedule_wakeup_bridge.py absent at the symlinked path → cake session's ScheduleWakeup hook errored → user pinged across repos. Fixed acutely by syncing local to main (files present), but the fragility remains: a dev branch switch here can still break hooks everywhere.

TRADEOFF (the core decision): the symlink-to-working-tree EXISTS to give live-edit convenience (harness edits take effect instantly — this is the workflow-tooling repo). Robustness costs that convenience.

OPTIONS:
(A) Deploy harness as COPIES via INSTALL.sh — robust; harness edits need re-deploy (lose instant live-edit).
(B) Point ~/.claude/harness at a PINNED checkout/worktree of this repo at origin/main — robust + git-updatable via 'git -C <pinned> pull'; immune to the dev's active branch; harness edits need pull-to-deploy.
(C) Keep live-edit symlink + make harness hook invocations FAIL-SOFT (missing target -> no-op, never an error) — keeps convenience, removes the machine-wide error-spam symptom, but stale state silently disables a hook rather than erroring.

ACCEPTANCE: a branch switch (or checkout of a branch lacking a harness file) in the dev working copy must NOT break or error hooks in other repos; missing harness file degrades to silent no-op, not error.
notes:
IMPLEMENTED (live machine) 2026-06-01: created pinned checkout ~/.claude/.cws-pinned (full clone @ origin/main); reversibly repointed ALL 57 ~/.claude symlinks (25 hooks, 13 rules, 8 skills, 4 commands, 2 agents, harness/bin, harness/schemas, claude/bin, project-bootstrap.sh) from the live working tree to the pinned checkout (57/57 verified, restore manifest at ~/.claude/.cws-pin-restore.tsv). Helpers: ~/.claude/.cws-update.sh (git -C pinned merge --ff-only origin/main = deploy latest main to this machine) and ~/.claude/.cws-unpin.sh (reverse via restore manifest). Acceptance MET on live machine: a branch switch in the dev working tree can no longer break hooks in any repo (deploy reads from pinned checkout, not the dev branch). DX CHANGE: harness/hook edits now go live only after reaching main AND running .cws-update.sh (no longer instant). REMAINING (to fully close): codify the pinned-checkout model in INSTALL.sh so fresh installs/new machines use it instead of symlink-to-working-tree; ship the two helper scripts into the repo (scripts/) rather than living only in ~/.claude. That codification is a normal repo change (branch+PR).
Codification PR #8 (harden/install-pinned-checkout): INSTALL.sh now deploys from a pinned checkout by default, with --update (deploy latest main) and --dev (live-tree opt-out) modes; tests/test_install_pinned.sh proves pinned-default + --dev escape; README updated. Live machine was already hand-converted; this makes fresh installs/new machines inherit the model. ft1 closeable once #8 merges. NOTE: standalone ~/.claude/.cws-update.sh + .cws-unpin.sh remain as machine-local conveniences; INSTALL.sh --update/--dev are the canonical, versioned mechanisms.
close_reason: Harden harness deploy DONE: live machine converted to pinned checkout (57 symlinks repointed, reversible) earlier; codified in INSTALL.sh (pinned default + --update/--dev modes + tests/test_install_pinned.sh) via PR #8 (merged). Fresh installs now inherit the branch-safe model.

title: System-wide: every gate must produce persistent signal (gate-design Rule 2)
description:
Integrator panel's cross-cutting finding from the 14-gate audit: NO gate in this repo writes its decisions to a durable store. All signal lives in conversation context and evaporates with the session.

## Affected

All 14+ hooks in claude/hooks/. None write to beads, none append to a log file, none call `bd audit record`. There is no way to answer 'which gate fires most? for what? did behavior change after revision?' because the data doesn't exist.

## Recommended remediation

Per gate-design.md Rule 2, every gate must persist decisions to at least one of:

- A beads issue (for actionable findings)
- An append-only log file (for high-volume gates) — e.g., `.beads/.gate-decisions.jsonl` with `{gate, timestamp, decision, reason, command_excerpt}`
- `bd audit record` (for events that fit the audit subsystem)
- A waivers file (`.beads/.gate-waivers.jsonl`) keyed by gate name

## Approach

Most efficient implementation is probably a shared helper module `claude/hooks/_gate_signal.py` exposing a single function like `record(gate_name, decision, reason, extras)`. All hooks import and call it. This way the persistence shape is centralized and revisable.

## Acceptance

- `claude/hooks/_gate_signal.py` exists with a documented API
- At least one existing gate is updated to call `record()` end-to-end
- A query script can answer 'how many times did gate X fire in the last N days, and what were the reasons?' against the resulting log
close_reason: Superseded by openspec change gate-signal-persistence-foundation (created same session). The work is now tracked via openspec design.md + future /work-breakdown beads, not as a single bead under the audit epic. The signal-persistence foundation enables both the staleness feature's learning loop AND the other 9 audit-revisions; promoting it from one of 10 children to its own change reflects that scope and surfaces it for earlier execution.

title: validate_no_shirking.py — revise per gate audit (coercive, no learning loop)
description:
Adversary panel ranked this the highest-priority revise of the 14 audited gates: oldest active gate, 25 hardcoded regex 'shirking' phrases, no half-life, no learning loop.

## Audit findings (commit b4d2732 area)

- Repair: FAIL — denial says 'Run the failing test(s)' but doesn't name which pattern matched; user has no documented unblock path other than typing magic approval words.
- Internal transparency: FAIL — `_BLOCK_BODY` asserts duty ('You own the OUTCOME') without naming what failure mode it prevents or linking outcome-ownership.md.
- Global transparency: FAIL — gate's relationship to outcome-ownership.md not referenced anywhere agent-visible.
- Flexibility: PARTIAL — undocumented waiver path (user approval words), no reason capture, produces zero signal back to the rule.
- Failure mode: coercive, drifting toward mock — phrase-matching trains agents to launder the same intent through synonyms.

## Recommended fix

Per gate-design.md Rules 1, 2, 3:

1. Every denial names the *category* matched ('deferral', 'attribution-deflection', 'infra-blame') and links the rationale section in outcome-ownership.md.
2. Waiver path is documented in the denial text itself; reason captured to a .beads/.gate-waivers.jsonl with the matched pattern and the user's override reason.
3. Add an explicit quarterly half-life: a small script that reports which patterns fire most over the last 90 days; prune dead ones.

## Acceptance

- Denial message includes the matched category name and a one-line pointer to outcome-ownership.md
- Waiver reasons (when user overrides) persist to a durable log
- A separate revision audit script exists and is documented in the rule
close_reason: validate_no_shirking revised: 33 regex patterns categorized into 8 named anti-pattern classes (pre-existing, attribution-deflection, unrelated, infrastructure-blame, deferral, scope-limitation, acceptance-evasion, dismissal); denial message names the matched category with a one-line rationale and links to outcome-ownership.md; signal capture at all 3 decision branches including waiver-accepted entries that capture user-overrides as labeled training data. The half-life mechanism (gate-design.md Operating Rule 1) is now mechanically queryable via gate_signal_query.py --gate validate_no_shirking. Adversary's full recommendation implemented. This is the highest-priority gate revise from the audit and the last open bead in epic claude-workflow-setup-3ky.

title: NS-2 verdict: does watermark under-block a multi-session UNCLAIMED contract-less molecule? (manifest-companion trigger)
description: Design residual (openspec/changes/gate-session-scope-model/design.md). The watermark treats beads created before session-start as backlog. NARROW risk: a molecule worked across sessions WITHOUT a claim AND WITHOUT a contract → its leaves predate today's watermark → read as backlog → premature spin-down with ready siblings (the exact bug the harness prevents). IMMUNE cases: claimed molecules (covered by root-walk task-mode path regardless of timestamp); contract-bearing sessions (contract.created_at watermark). DECISION: if this intersection is common in practice, add the explicit manifest (Option D) as a COMPANION to the watermark (NOT a replacement) — fully designed as the deferred fallback in design.md; re-ping bureaucracy (manifest re-incurs the value-validation story). Adversary's explicit NS-2 verdict was the panel's one unfinished item; treat real-world half-life signal (scope_backlog_allow rate on resumed molecules) as the trigger.

title: Review discovery: work-intake-enforcement
description:
Phase: DESIGN (decision point)

Two dispatches before presenting to the user:

1. ADVERSARIAL REVIEW — dispatch adversarial-reviewer subagent (isolated, no conversation history) to review the design. Sees ONLY the design content.

2. QA REVIEW (feature/epic only, skip for rapid) — dispatch a QA roundtable that starts from the Success Criteria section and works backward: 'What could prevent each success criterion from being achieved?'
   - feature: 3 named expert agents (TeamCreate + name + team_name), different domain lenses
   - epic: 5-8 named expert agents with domain-specific lenses (security, compliance, operations, developer experience, financial correctness, etc.)
   Agents argue via SendMessage. Their findings can change the scope — add non-goals, revise the walking skeleton, surface gaps in the success criteria themselves.
   Do NOT shutdown QA agents until the user explicitly says they're done.

Then present to the user:
- The adversarial reviewer's findings (BLOCK/CONCERN/NOTE)
- The QA roundtable's findings — especially any success criteria that the design doesn't address
- A plain-language summary of the design:
  - The success criteria and whether they're observable/falsifiable
  - The riskiest assumption and whether it's falsifiable
  - The walking skeleton scope (1-3 tasks, 30-60 min each)
  - The non-goals (are they specific enough?)

If either review found BLOCKs or unaddressed success criteria, recommend revision before approval.

User approves by saying 'looks good' or 'approved' — resolve the gate.
User can say 'revise' to send back to discovery.

Do NOT show gate IDs or bd commands to the user.

title: Brainstorm: work-intake-enforcement
description:
Phase: THINK

Challenge whether this work should happen at all.

Problem: Non-trivial implementation work can be performed and merged without entering the repo's QA/TDD process, because enforcement checks artifact presence at the end rather than routing the work at the start.

Dispatch a team of named expert agents (TeamCreate + name + team_name) to challenge the idea from different angles. Use techniques from ~/.claude/skills/discovery/references/brainstorming-methods.csv and ~/.claude/skills/discovery/references/elicitation-methods.csv. Pick 1-2 techniques appropriate to the problem. Good defaults: First Principles Analysis, Pre-mortem Analysis, Assumption Reversal, Question Storming.

Agents should communicate via SendMessage and argue with each other. Relay their questions and findings to the user.

Possible outcomes (all tracked in beads):
1. BUILD — proceed to discovery step
2. DON'T BUILD — close molecule with reason. Record why.
3. DEFER — bd defer with context. Surface later when conditions change.
4. RECLASSIFY — this is actually a bug/chore. Close molecule, pour mol-rapid instead.
5. SPLIT — too big. Break into child epics first, then pour separate molecules.
6. DELEGATE — not our work. Record who owns it.
7. NEED MORE INFO — create a spike (type=spike) to investigate first.

For outcomes 2-7: close this molecule with --reason explaining the decision.
For outcome 1: close this step and proceed to discovery.

Do NOT show bd commands to the user. Present the challenge conversationally.
Do NOT shutdown agents until the user explicitly says they're done.
close_reason: BRAINSTORM OUTCOME: DON'T BUILD the proposed work-intake gate; RECLASSIFY/reframe to prevention-first. 3-agent adversarial panel (root-cause/contrarian/pre-mortem) + user steering converged. ROOT CAUSE: self-authored oracle (the agent that does the work authors the signal that judges it). PRINCIPLE: an agent cannot be its own independence boundary in-session (owns its files, workflow script, bead edits, sub-agent prompts); two escapes only = (a) self-validating oracle, (b) out-of-session boundary (human/separate parent). A PreToolUse hook can see tool metadata, never oracle-authorship provenance -> independence is NOT hook-enforceable (category error). VERIFIED: tdd_gate 461 ask/164 allow (74% interrupt), validate_no_shirking 766 deny/39 waiver (95% evade) -> a new blocking gate inherits the evade reflex. USER'S DECISIVE CORRECTION (stated 3x): any check at PR/first-edit/commit is DETECTION after the defect exists; enforcement belongs at PREVENTION time. PREVENTION-FIRST DISPOSITION: verbatim refactor (cake's class) -> do the move MECHANICALLY (LSP/Serena move-symbol or AST codemod) so transcription errors are unrepresentable by construction; behavior-changing work -> oracle authored by an independent boundary BEFORE the implementer starts (oracle-first sequencing). Detection gates (commit-time relocation+AST proof, coverage-delta) demoted to fail-closed BACKSTOPS, not the mechanism. Molecule was mis-framed ('work-intake-enforcement' presupposed intake enforcement was the answer; it was not).

title: Review skeleton results: work-intake-enforcement
description:
Phase: VALIDATE (decision point)

Dispatch an adversarial-reviewer subagent (isolated, no conversation history) to review the skeleton code. The reviewer sees ONLY the changed files and test results.

Also dispatch a QA agent (TeamCreate + name + team_name) alongside the adversarial reviewer to produce system-level spec issues:
- Reads the design doc '## Risks and Edge Cases' section
- Reads the skeleton's changed files to understand the end-to-end flow that now exists
- Creates spec issues (--type spec, --parent <epic-id>) for integration scenarios that only become visible now that the skeleton exists: cross-component data flow, error propagation paths, concurrent access scenarios
- Scenarios must be testable against the skeleton code that exists RIGHT NOW — do not spec integration paths that require execute-full work to exist. If a cross-component path depends on a component not yet built, tag the spec issue :spec_only and note which execute-full task must complete before it becomes testable.
- Applies the same WHEN/THEN observable-outcome standard and scope circuit breaker as work-breakdown
- Does NOT re-cover what task-level spec issues already cover
These system-level specs become additional tasks if they reveal unaddressed risk.

This gate is CONDITIONAL:

- If all skeleton tasks closed as DONE with no concerns AND the adversarial reviewer found no BLOCKs:
  Auto-resolve this gate and proceed to full execution.
  Tell the user: 'Skeleton validated cleanly. Proceeding to full build.'

- If any skeleton task was DONE_WITH_CONCERNS or BLOCKED, or the reviewer found BLOCKs:
  Present the concerns + reviewer findings and ask the user to decide:
  'The skeleton found issues: [summary]. Proceed, revise, or stop?'
  User approves → resolve gate.
  User says revise → create amendment tasks, update design.
  User says stop → close molecule with reason.

Do NOT show gate IDs or bd commands to the user.

title: Discovery: work-intake-enforcement
description:
Phase: DESIGN

Run /discovery to produce an adversarial design for work-intake-enforcement.

Problem: Non-trivial implementation work can be performed and merged without entering the repo's QA/TDD process, because enforcement checks artifact presence at the end rather than routing the work at the start.

Before drafting, dispatch parallel named research agents (TeamCreate + name + team_name) to investigate:
- Existing code patterns relevant to this feature
- Similar past designs in docs/plans/ or openspec/changes/
- External API docs or constraints that affect the design

Agents report findings via SendMessage. Use their findings to inform the design.

The design must include:
1. Problem Statement
2. Success Criteria — numbered, observable, falsifiable outcomes. Not 'what we build' but 'what changes in the world when this ships.' Each criterion answers: 'A stakeholder would look at this and say yes, the problem is solved, because [specific observable state].' Minimum 3 for feature, minimum 5 for epic. These are the PRIMARY OUTPUT of discovery — everything else derives from them.
3. Non-Goals (min 3 — name what they lock in, not just what's excluded)
4. Riskiest Assumption (falsifiable bet with liveness test)
5. Walking Skeleton (1-3 tasks, 30-60 min each — each task traces to a success criterion)
6. Proof of Delivery (the single most important success criterion, stated as a sentence)
7. Anti-Metrics (what gets worse if we succeed wrong)
8. Future Increments

Output: openspec/changes/work-intake-enforcement/ (design.md, specs/, tasks.md).
The /discovery skill handles this automatically via the OpenSpec CLI.

When complete, close this step. The review gate will activate.

title: Work breakdown: work-intake-enforcement
description:
Phase: DESIGN

Run /work-breakdown to create specs and tasks in beads.

Input: The design from the openspec/changes/work-intake-enforcement/ artifacts.

SCOPE CIRCUIT BREAKER — apply before creating any spec issues:
- Count public API endpoints + critical state transitions in the feature scope
- P0 spec issues ≤ 2× that count
- P1 spec issues ≤ 3× P0 count
- Total spec issues ≤ 10× distinct behaviors
- Any spec issue that would exceed these ceilings → reclassify to NOTE in the design doc, not a beads issue
This prevents spec inflation from compounding across steps.

For multi-component features, dispatch parallel named agents (TeamCreate + name + team_name) — one per component — to draft spec issues simultaneously. Agents share findings via SendMessage to ensure cross-component contracts are consistent.

1. Create SPEC ISSUES as children of the molecule root epic:
   - bd create --type spec --title '[SPEC] component-name' --parent <epic-id>
   - Store WHEN/THEN behavioral contracts in the --acceptance field
   - WHEN/THEN must describe observable outcomes, not implementation steps:
     WRONG: 'WHEN cloneRecord() is called, THEN start_date is set'
     RIGHT: 'WHEN a campaign is cloned, THEN campaign.start_date equals template.start_date'
   - REQUIRED: every spec issue must have at least one named requirement ID (e.g., #cached-token-lookup, #campaign-cloner-date-preservation). These IDs are the longitudinal tracking key — test files that cover a scenario tag the ID in a comment (e.g., '# covers #cached-token-lookup'), enabling gap queries without manual correlation. A spec issue with no named requirement ID cannot be queried for coverage drift.
   - Specs are reusable — multiple tasks can reference the same spec via --spec-id

2. TESTING REVIEW — after spec agents draft --acceptance fields, dispatch testing agents that work from the SPECS (not success criteria — QA already covered outcomes at review-discovery).

   SCALING:
   - rapid: single testing agent, 1-2 scenarios per spec issue
   - feature: 2-3 named testing agents (TeamCreate + name + team_name) focused on: edge cases, error handling, data integrity
   - epic: 3-5 named testing agents with technical lenses (concurrency, performance, security boundaries, data migration, API contract stability)

   ORIENTATION: Each testing agent reads the spec issues' --acceptance fields and the actual codebase, then asks: 'What behavior is specified but not yet covered by acceptance criteria? What edge cases does this code path have that no criterion addresses?'

   DEDUPLICATION (before adding anything to --acceptance):
   - Layer 1: OpenSpec behavioral contracts — don't restate
   - Layer 2: Existing test inventory + test infrastructure — don't restate
   - Layer 3: Codebase — verify scenarios describe IMPLEMENTED behavior, not aspirational
   Only genuine gaps get added to --acceptance fields.

   QUALITY GATES on --acceptance fields:
   - THEN clause must contain a comparison operator + specific value (falsifiability check)
   - REFUSES proxy metrics ('not_to be_empty', 'response code 200') — hard stop, requires rewrite
   - Scenarios must assert OUTCOMES, not INTERFACES
   - Does NOT generate test files — enriches --acceptance fields only

   The developer reads --acceptance before writing their RED test. This field IS the spec.

   NOTE ON PARALLELISM: Steps 2 (QA second-author pass) and 3 (create task issues) run in parallel — spec enrichment does not depend on task creation. Both must complete before step 4 (present to user for approval). Do not run them sequentially.

   Additionally, produce ONE test pattern catalog for the feature (not per-task). This is ~20 lines covering:
   - How this codebase writes API request tests (auth setup, call pattern, response parsing)
   - How this codebase writes job/service tests (invocation pattern, factory dependencies)
   - Which factories exist and their key attributes
   - Any factories that do NOT yet exist and must be created as part of the feature
   Store the catalog in the epic's --description or as a comment on the epic issue. Isolated implementing agents read this at task-start to avoid redundant discovery of codebase patterns. This is orientation material, not a contract — implementing agents may deviate based on what they find.

3. Create TASK ISSUES as children of the molecule root epic:
   - Each task references its spec: --spec-id <spec-issue-id>
   - Outcome-based acceptance criteria (done when X, not when Y)
   - Failure modes (how this task fails)
   - Scope boundaries (what's NOT in this task)

4. Apply READY FOR DEVELOPMENT STANDARD to every task:
   - Actionable: file path + specific action
   - Logical: tasks ordered by dependency (use bd dep)
   - Testable: Given/When/Then covering happy path + edge cases
   - Complete: no placeholders, no TBDs
   - Self-Contained: a fresh Claude session can implement without conversation history

Label walking skeleton tasks with 'skeleton' and set priority P1.
Label remaining tasks with P2-P3.

Present the task list to the user for approval BEFORE creating beads issues.
When approved and tasks are created, close this step.

title: Execute remaining tasks: work-intake-enforcement
description:
Phase: BUILD

BEFORE dispatching implementing agents, run a SYSTEM-LEVEL QA PASS (this is Tier 3 — cross-component integration specs):
- Dispatch a QA agent (TeamCreate + name + team_name) that reads: the validated skeleton code, the design doc's cross-component flow descriptions, and all existing spec issues
- Generates system-level spec issues for integration scenarios that span multiple components: the full activation pipeline, cross-org isolation, error propagation across service boundaries, concurrent access paths
- This is the right moment — the skeleton has proven the architecture works, all components will exist by end of execute-full, and no implementing agents have started yet. Post-skeleton was too early (minimal code); end-of-execute-full is too late (chicken-and-egg with spec audit)
- Applies the same WHEN/THEN observable-outcome standard and scope circuit breaker as work-breakdown
- These system-level spec issues become tasks in the first or second wave

Acceptance criteria define OUTCOMES, not test implementations. An implementing agent satisfies a criterion if their test verifies the same observable outcome — the assertion code may differ from what the criterion literally describes. 'campaign.start_date equals template.start_date' can be verified as `expect(campaign.reload.start_date).to eq(template.start_date)` or any equivalent assertion. Criteria constrain the outcome; the developer chooses the verification approach.

Run /beads-execution to dispatch parallel named agents (TeamCreate + name + team_name) for ALL remaining tasks. Dispatch in dependency-aware waves — all unblocked tasks in a wave get their own named agent working simultaneously.

Query to find remaining tasks:
  bd list --parent <molecule-root-id> --status open
  (skeleton tasks are already closed)

If agents discover assumption failures during execution:
- Surface via BLOCKED or DONE_WITH_CONCERNS
- Create amendment tasks if the design needs revision
- When an amendment updates a spec issue's --acceptance field: (1) the amendment task description MUST note 'Spec issue [id] --acceptance criteria have changed — re-read before the next RED test'; (2) all task issues referencing this spec via --spec-id are affected — list them explicitly so the developer knows which tasks are touched. Task amendments that do NOT change --acceptance (e.g., implementation approach, file location, effort) leave existing test criteria valid.
- Do not silently work around problems

When all tasks complete:
1. SPEC AUDIT — Dispatch a named agent to systematically walk through EVERY spec scenario (bd list --parent <molecule-root-id> --type spec). For each WHEN/THEN contract, verify the implementation satisfies it. This is not a code review — it is a spec-to-implementation gap check. Any unmet scenario becomes a new task.
2. Dispatch adversarial-reviewer subagent (isolated, no conversation history) on the complete implementation — all changed files, all test results.
3. Run proof-of-delivery verification:
   - Execute the actual workflow the user cares about
   - Check against anti-metrics from the design
   - Verify the proof of delivery sentence is satisfied
4. Present spec audit gaps + reviewer findings + verification results to the user.

Only close this step when ALL spec scenarios pass, the adversarial reviewer has reported, AND proof of delivery is verified.

title: Harness: task-mode gate can't distinguish session-decided work from unrelated backlog (nudges premature stop)
description: Observed 2026-06-01 (long superpowers session). The continuation-harness task-mode gate ('tasks_remain_in_queue' / 'drain the queue OR ask user to release') cannot tell (a) freshly-filed beads that ARE this session's just-agreed work from (b) unrelated backlog from other sessions. Result: it repeatedly nudged toward 'ask the user to release' while real decided work sat ready, and the agent took the bait — treating 'filed the beads' as a terminal state instead of executing. This INVERTS the harness's own outcome-bias goal (it rewarded filing, an intermediate artifact, over executing the outcome). Fix direction: let the gate treat beads created/decided in the current session (or tagged to the session goal) as in-scope-to-drain, not deferrable backlog. RELATED false-positive cluster from the same session: validate_no_shirking fired repeatedly on prose-only docs edits (no code) and even on the agent's own explanation of a prior false-positive (recursive 'CI failure' / 'unrelated' / 'infrastructure-blame' keyword matches); and ScheduleWakeup still doesn't satisfy the Stop gate (see 0wg) / validate_no_shirking certainty-idiom over-fire (see x2o).
acceptance_criteria: Gate distinguishes session-scope beads from unrelated backlog (or a documented mechanism to mark them), so executing freshly-decided work is the rewarded path; validate_no_shirking does not fire on prose-only docs edits or on meta-discussion of its own matches. Cross-ref 0wg, x2o.
close_reason: Folded into claude-workflow-setup-858 (Cluster 2). Same root design question — gate can't model session scope vs unrelated backlog — now consolidated with the ignored-block/rationalization mode for the design panel. f6q's full text, acceptance criteria, and cross-refs (0wg, x2o) are preserved in 858. Not lost work; deduplicated.

title: Disconnect from superpowers (delete + redirect to own skills; NOT vendor)
description: Decision (2026-05-31, user-led 8/8 con review): FULL DISCONNECT from the superpowers plugin. Rationale: the repo matches/exceeds superpowers on every skill it uses (edge is in rules+harness, not skills); the 2-3 gaps (receiving-code-review, writing-skills, standalone systematic-debugging) are UNUSED and reversibly re-installable; the only live executed edge is brainstorming:230->writing-plans. Method = deletion + redirect to the repo's OWN skills (work-breakdown, own review prompts, inline finish) — NOT vendoring/copying (so it does not trip the bloat guardrail). Full analysis + con-by-con rulings: docs/analysis/superpowers-vendor-vs-wrap.md. NOTE: this supersedes the panel's earlier 'keep-and-override + pin + detection-test' rec (which rested on the disproven 'upstream churns weekly on coupled files'); with full disconnect there is no link to pin or monitor.
design: No vendoring, no pin, no drift-detection-test (nothing to monitor once disconnected). The ONE-TIME completeness assertion (grep: no superpowers: refs remain) doubles as a regression guard. Sequence the brainstorming edits under a SINGLE OWNER coordinated with why-drilling (a2n) and the mol-feature consolidation (ea3) to avoid triplicate-authoring brainstorming/SKILL.md Sections 2/5.
acceptance_criteria: DONE when: zero 'superpowers:' references remain in claude/ (grep returns empty) AND the repo's own skills/redirects cover every previously-referenced function (brainstorm flow, planning via work-breakdown, worktrees via bd, finish via inline PR-only, code-review via own prompts) AND the live brainstorming step-number bug is fixed. NOT done when children merely closed — verify by the grep + by pouring mol-feature and confirming brainstorm/discovery/finish run correctly with no superpowers dependency.
close_reason: Full code disconnect complete + verified: zero superpowers: refs in production skills/hooks (committed regression-guard test_no_superpowers_refs.py passes; 551 hook tests green); all redirects to own skills; live brainstorming bug fixed. c1v gap-skills decided (decline/reinstallable). Plugin remains installed but inert/unreferenced; physical uninstall optional.

title: Work breakdown: beads-clone-shared-truth
description:
Phase: DESIGN

Run /work-breakdown to create specs and tasks in beads.

Input: The design from the openspec/changes/beads-clone-shared-truth/ artifacts.

SCOPE CIRCUIT BREAKER — apply before creating any spec issues:
- Count public API endpoints + critical state transitions in the feature scope
- P0 spec issues ≤ 2× that count
- P1 spec issues ≤ 3× P0 count
- Total spec issues ≤ 10× distinct behaviors
- Any spec issue that would exceed these ceilings → reclassify to NOTE in the design doc, not a beads issue
This prevents spec inflation from compounding across steps.

For multi-component features, dispatch parallel named agents (TeamCreate + name + team_name) — one per component — to draft spec issues simultaneously. Agents share findings via SendMessage to ensure cross-component contracts are consistent.

1. Create SPEC ISSUES as children of the molecule root epic:
   - bd create --type spec --title '[SPEC] component-name' --parent <epic-id>
   - Store WHEN/THEN behavioral contracts in the --acceptance field
   - WHEN/THEN must describe observable outcomes, not implementation steps:
     WRONG: 'WHEN cloneRecord() is called, THEN start_date is set'
     RIGHT: 'WHEN a campaign is cloned, THEN campaign.start_date equals template.start_date'
   - REQUIRED: every spec issue must have at least one named requirement ID (e.g., #cached-token-lookup, #campaign-cloner-date-preservation). These IDs are the longitudinal tracking key — test files that cover a scenario tag the ID in a comment (e.g., '# covers #cached-token-lookup'), enabling gap queries without manual correlation. A spec issue with no named requirement ID cannot be queried for coverage drift.
   - Specs are reusable — multiple tasks can reference the same spec via --spec-id

2. TESTING REVIEW — after spec agents draft --acceptance fields, dispatch testing agents that work from the SPECS (not success criteria — QA already covered outcomes at review-discovery).

   SCALING:
   - rapid: single testing agent, 1-2 scenarios per spec issue
   - feature: 2-3 named testing agents (TeamCreate + name + team_name) focused on: edge cases, error handling, data integrity
   - epic: 3-5 named testing agents with technical lenses (concurrency, performance, security boundaries, data migration, API contract stability)

   ORIENTATION: Each testing agent reads the spec issues' --acceptance fields and the actual codebase, then asks: 'What behavior is specified but not yet covered by acceptance criteria? What edge cases does this code path have that no criterion addresses?'

   DEDUPLICATION (before adding anything to --acceptance):
   - Layer 1: OpenSpec behavioral contracts — don't restate
   - Layer 2: Existing test inventory + test infrastructure — don't restate
   - Layer 3: Codebase — verify scenarios describe IMPLEMENTED behavior, not aspirational
   Only genuine gaps get added to --acceptance fields.

   QUALITY GATES on --acceptance fields:
   - THEN clause must contain a comparison operator + specific value (falsifiability check)
   - REFUSES proxy metrics ('not_to be_empty', 'response code 200') — hard stop, requires rewrite
   - Scenarios must assert OUTCOMES, not INTERFACES
   - Does NOT generate test files — enriches --acceptance fields only

   The developer reads --acceptance before writing their RED test. This field IS the spec.

   NOTE ON PARALLELISM: Steps 2 (QA second-author pass) and 3 (create task issues) run in parallel — spec enrichment does not depend on task creation. Both must complete before step 4 (present to user for approval). Do not run them sequentially.

   Additionally, produce ONE test pattern catalog for the feature (not per-task). This is ~20 lines covering:
   - How this codebase writes API request tests (auth setup, call pattern, response parsing)
   - How this codebase writes job/service tests (invocation pattern, factory dependencies)
   - Which factories exist and their key attributes
   - Any factories that do NOT yet exist and must be created as part of the feature
   Store the catalog in the epic's --description or as a comment on the epic issue. Isolated implementing agents read this at task-start to avoid redundant discovery of codebase patterns. This is orientation material, not a contract — implementing agents may deviate based on what they find.

3. Create TASK ISSUES as children of the molecule root epic:
   - Each task references its spec: --spec-id <spec-issue-id>
   - Outcome-based acceptance criteria (done when X, not when Y)
   - Failure modes (how this task fails)
   - Scope boundaries (what's NOT in this task)

4. Apply READY FOR DEVELOPMENT STANDARD to every task:
   - Actionable: file path + specific action
   - Logical: tasks ordered by dependency (use bd dep)
   - Testable: Given/When/Then covering happy path + edge cases
   - Complete: no placeholders, no TBDs
   - Self-Contained: a fresh Claude session can implement without conversation history

Label walking skeleton tasks with 'skeleton' and set priority P1.
Label remaining tasks with P2-P3.

Present the task list to the user for approval BEFORE creating beads issues.
When approved and tasks are created, close this step.

title: mol-feature
description: Full feature workflow: brainstorm → design → review → breakdown → skeleton → validation → build → ceremony retro → outcome check. Gates pause for human review at natural decision points. Learning loops close the feedback cycle.
notes: PARKED 2026-06-01 pending beads v1.0.6 (user decision). Review-discovery REJECTED the committed-JSONL design with 3 grounded blockers: B1 default bd export EXCLUDES memories (0/5; security-sensitive) so clone rehydrates 0 memories + the proposed skeleton would falsely pass; B2 concurrent same-bead edits write git conflict markers into issues.jsonl and bd import HARD-FAILS ('invalid character <') — no merge driver exists; B3 interactions.jsonl is tracked but bd export cannot emit it (no round-trip path). Strategic reframe (reviewer N2 + my analysis): Dolt-remote (Path B) dissolves B1/B2/B3 via cell-level merges + full-DB fidelity, and refs/dolt/* on origin satisfies 'lives with the repo' after the setup step we're building anyway. BUT Path B depends on multi-machine bd dolt sync, which v1.0.5 can unrecoverably corrupt and is only fixed in pending v1.0.6. RESUME TRIGGER: v1.0.6 released + dolt-sync fix verified. On resume: spike Dolt-remote round-trip (push refs/dolt to origin, fresh clone + fetch-refspec + bd dolt pull, verify full truth incl memories/interactions + a concurrent-edit merge); if green commit Path B, else fall back to Path A (committed-JSONL + custom union merge driver + memories security decision). Safe-now work that does NOT depend on sync model (clone-bootstrap script + announce-confirm auto-rehydrate; worktree discipline) was deferred with this park but could be split out if relief is needed sooner. Design docs: openspec/changes/beads-clone-shared-truth/.

title: Brainstorm: beads-clone-shared-truth
description:
Phase: THINK

Challenge whether this work should happen at all.

Problem: A fresh clone of this beads repo cannot see or work the shared bead truth: the committed issues.jsonl is present but bd reports 'no database found' until a manual bd init+import+hooks-install bootstrap, and ongoing use suffers export churn that blocks git operations, branch-switch state reverts, and noisy auto-resync commits — so the team cannot reliably work from a shared, in-repo source of truth.

Dispatch a team of named expert agents (TeamCreate + name + team_name) to challenge the idea from different angles. Use techniques from ~/.claude/skills/discovery/references/brainstorming-methods.csv and ~/.claude/skills/discovery/references/elicitation-methods.csv. Pick 1-2 techniques appropriate to the problem. Good defaults: First Principles Analysis, Pre-mortem Analysis, Assumption Reversal, Question Storming.

Agents should communicate via SendMessage and argue with each other. Relay their questions and findings to the user.

Possible outcomes (all tracked in beads):
1. BUILD — proceed to discovery step
2. DON'T BUILD — close molecule with reason. Record why.
3. DEFER — bd defer with context. Surface later when conditions change.
4. RECLASSIFY — this is actually a bug/chore. Close molecule, pour mol-rapid instead.
5. SPLIT — too big. Break into child epics first, then pour separate molecules.
6. DELEGATE — not our work. Record who owns it.
7. NEED MORE INFO — create a spike (type=spike) to investigate first.

For outcomes 2-7: close this molecule with --reason explaining the decision.
For outcome 1: close this step and proceed to discovery.

Do NOT show bd commands to the user. Present the challenge conversationally.
Do NOT shutdown agents until the user explicitly says they're done.
close_reason: Brainstorm complete. Framing: irreducible conflict = bd needs a binary Dolt DB (git-ignored, undiffable) but shared-truth-in-clone needs committed text → reconcile via clone-time rehydration from the already-committed issues.jsonl (NOT a Dolt-remote migration; refs/dolt/* aren't fetched by plain git clone). Decisions: (1) bootstrap UX = repo setup script + SessionStart auto-rehydrate; (2) scope = this repo first, then standardize to other JSONL-tracking repos. Riskiest assumption = issues.jsonl is lossless (clone+init+import reproduces identical issues/statuses/deps/memories); walking skeleton must diff rehydrated state vs source.

title: Gate: human discovery-approved
description: Async gate for step review-discovery

title: Review discovery: beads-clone-shared-truth
description:
Phase: DESIGN (decision point)

Two dispatches before presenting to the user:

1. ADVERSARIAL REVIEW — dispatch adversarial-reviewer subagent (isolated, no conversation history) to review the design. Sees ONLY the design content.

2. QA REVIEW (feature/epic only, skip for rapid) — dispatch a QA roundtable that starts from the Success Criteria section and works backward: 'What could prevent each success criterion from being achieved?'
   - feature: 3 named expert agents (TeamCreate + name + team_name), different domain lenses
   - epic: 5-8 named expert agents with domain-specific lenses (security, compliance, operations, developer experience, financial correctness, etc.)
   Agents argue via SendMessage. Their findings can change the scope — add non-goals, revise the walking skeleton, surface gaps in the success criteria themselves.
   Do NOT shutdown QA agents until the user explicitly says they're done.

Then present to the user:
- The adversarial reviewer's findings (BLOCK/CONCERN/NOTE)
- The QA roundtable's findings — especially any success criteria that the design doesn't address
- A plain-language summary of the design:
  - The success criteria and whether they're observable/falsifiable
  - The riskiest assumption and whether it's falsifiable
  - The walking skeleton scope (1-3 tasks, 30-60 min each)
  - The non-goals (are they specific enough?)

If either review found BLOCKs or unaddressed success criteria, recommend revision before approval.

User approves by saying 'looks good' or 'approved' — resolve the gate.
User can say 'revise' to send back to discovery.

Do NOT show gate IDs or bd commands to the user.

title: Discovery: beads-clone-shared-truth
description:
Phase: DESIGN

Run /discovery to produce an adversarial design for beads-clone-shared-truth.

Problem: A fresh clone of this beads repo cannot see or work the shared bead truth: the committed issues.jsonl is present but bd reports 'no database found' until a manual bd init+import+hooks-install bootstrap, and ongoing use suffers export churn that blocks git operations, branch-switch state reverts, and noisy auto-resync commits — so the team cannot reliably work from a shared, in-repo source of truth.

Before drafting, dispatch parallel named research agents (TeamCreate + name + team_name) to investigate:
- Existing code patterns relevant to this feature
- Similar past designs in docs/plans/ or openspec/changes/
- External API docs or constraints that affect the design

Agents report findings via SendMessage. Use their findings to inform the design.

The design must include:
1. Problem Statement
2. Success Criteria — numbered, observable, falsifiable outcomes. Not 'what we build' but 'what changes in the world when this ships.' Each criterion answers: 'A stakeholder would look at this and say yes, the problem is solved, because [specific observable state].' Minimum 3 for feature, minimum 5 for epic. These are the PRIMARY OUTPUT of discovery — everything else derives from them.
3. Non-Goals (min 3 — name what they lock in, not just what's excluded)
4. Riskiest Assumption (falsifiable bet with liveness test)
5. Walking Skeleton (1-3 tasks, 30-60 min each — each task traces to a success criterion)
6. Proof of Delivery (the single most important success criterion, stated as a sentence)
7. Anti-Metrics (what gets worse if we succeed wrong)
8. Future Increments

Output: openspec/changes/beads-clone-shared-truth/ (design.md, specs/, tasks.md).
The /discovery skill handles this automatically via the OpenSpec CLI.

When complete, close this step. The review gate will activate.
close_reason: Discovery complete. Wrote openspec/changes/beads-clone-shared-truth/{problem-framing,design}.md. Approach: rehydrate DB from committed issues.jsonl via (1) scripts/beads-setup.sh + (2) SessionStart auto-bootstrap; ongoing-sync hygiene + worktree discipline for friction. Riskiest assumption tested in a fresh clone: bd init+import rehydrated 38/38 issues, statuses preserved — issue+status losslessness confirmed; dep-edge+memory fidelity is the open item the walking skeleton (mol-w0q) closes. Non-goals: no Dolt-remote migration, no gitignoring jsonl, no committing the binary DB.

title: Review skeleton results: beads-clone-shared-truth
description:
Phase: VALIDATE (decision point)

Dispatch an adversarial-reviewer subagent (isolated, no conversation history) to review the skeleton code. The reviewer sees ONLY the changed files and test results.

Also dispatch a QA agent (TeamCreate + name + team_name) alongside the adversarial reviewer to produce system-level spec issues:
- Reads the design doc '## Risks and Edge Cases' section
- Reads the skeleton's changed files to understand the end-to-end flow that now exists
- Creates spec issues (--type spec, --parent <epic-id>) for integration scenarios that only become visible now that the skeleton exists: cross-component data flow, error propagation paths, concurrent access scenarios
- Scenarios must be testable against the skeleton code that exists RIGHT NOW — do not spec integration paths that require execute-full work to exist. If a cross-component path depends on a component not yet built, tag the spec issue :spec_only and note which execute-full task must complete before it becomes testable.
- Applies the same WHEN/THEN observable-outcome standard and scope circuit breaker as work-breakdown
- Does NOT re-cover what task-level spec issues already cover
These system-level specs become additional tasks if they reveal unaddressed risk.

This gate is CONDITIONAL:

- If all skeleton tasks closed as DONE with no concerns AND the adversarial reviewer found no BLOCKs:
  Auto-resolve this gate and proceed to full execution.
  Tell the user: 'Skeleton validated cleanly. Proceeding to full build.'

- If any skeleton task was DONE_WITH_CONCERNS or BLOCKED, or the reviewer found BLOCKs:
  Present the concerns + reviewer findings and ask the user to decide:
  'The skeleton found issues: [summary]. Proceed, revise, or stop?'
  User approves → resolve gate.
  User says revise → create amendment tasks, update design.
  User says stop → close molecule with reason.

Do NOT show gate IDs or bd commands to the user.

title: Execute remaining tasks: beads-clone-shared-truth
description:
Phase: BUILD

BEFORE dispatching implementing agents, run a SYSTEM-LEVEL QA PASS (this is Tier 3 — cross-component integration specs):
- Dispatch a QA agent (TeamCreate + name + team_name) that reads: the validated skeleton code, the design doc's cross-component flow descriptions, and all existing spec issues
- Generates system-level spec issues for integration scenarios that span multiple components: the full activation pipeline, cross-org isolation, error propagation across service boundaries, concurrent access paths
- This is the right moment — the skeleton has proven the architecture works, all components will exist by end of execute-full, and no implementing agents have started yet. Post-skeleton was too early (minimal code); end-of-execute-full is too late (chicken-and-egg with spec audit)
- Applies the same WHEN/THEN observable-outcome standard and scope circuit breaker as work-breakdown
- These system-level spec issues become tasks in the first or second wave

Acceptance criteria define OUTCOMES, not test implementations. An implementing agent satisfies a criterion if their test verifies the same observable outcome — the assertion code may differ from what the criterion literally describes. 'campaign.start_date equals template.start_date' can be verified as `expect(campaign.reload.start_date).to eq(template.start_date)` or any equivalent assertion. Criteria constrain the outcome; the developer chooses the verification approach.

Run /beads-execution to dispatch parallel named agents (TeamCreate + name + team_name) for ALL remaining tasks. Dispatch in dependency-aware waves — all unblocked tasks in a wave get their own named agent working simultaneously.

Query to find remaining tasks:
  bd list --parent <molecule-root-id> --status open
  (skeleton tasks are already closed)

If agents discover assumption failures during execution:
- Surface via BLOCKED or DONE_WITH_CONCERNS
- Create amendment tasks if the design needs revision
- When an amendment updates a spec issue's --acceptance field: (1) the amendment task description MUST note 'Spec issue [id] --acceptance criteria have changed — re-read before the next RED test'; (2) all task issues referencing this spec via --spec-id are affected — list them explicitly so the developer knows which tasks are touched. Task amendments that do NOT change --acceptance (e.g., implementation approach, file location, effort) leave existing test criteria valid.
- Do not silently work around problems

When all tasks complete:
1. SPEC AUDIT — Dispatch a named agent to systematically walk through EVERY spec scenario (bd list --parent <molecule-root-id> --type spec). For each WHEN/THEN contract, verify the implementation satisfies it. This is not a code review — it is a spec-to-implementation gap check. Any unmet scenario becomes a new task.
2. Dispatch adversarial-reviewer subagent (isolated, no conversation history) on the complete implementation — all changed files, all test results.
3. Run proof-of-delivery verification:
   - Execute the actual workflow the user cares about
   - Check against anti-metrics from the design
   - Verify the proof of delivery sentence is satisfied
4. Present spec audit gaps + reviewer findings + verification results to the user.

Only close this step when ALL spec scenarios pass, the adversarial reviewer has reported, AND proof of delivery is verified.

title: Gate: human skeleton-validated
description: Async gate for step review-skeleton

title: Harness waker should be the firing authority for ScheduleWakeup (suppress runtime built-in fallback replay)
description: Follow-up to 0wg part 2. The harness now (a) bridges ScheduleWakeup -> scheduled.json so the Stop gate's path-2 fires, and (b) self-cancels a thread's ScheduleWakeup wakeups on verify-pass + prunes past entries on write. RESIDUAL: the Claude Code runtime's own built-in fallback firing is NOT driven by scheduled.json, so it can still re-inject a prompt for already-completed work. Complete firing-side fix = build the harness waker (continuation-harness.md 'launchd waker', v0.1+) and make it the firing authority, reading the now-pruned scheduled.json as source of truth. Until then the residual is documented in schedule_wakeup_bridge.py.

title: Observe Depth-1 Probe in real use (1-2 weeks); decide ship/kill
description: Use in real work, populate the signal log. Kill condition: high empty-fire on lookups OR user disables -> do NOT ship Depth-1; keep only opt-in deeper tiers. Ref §4.
acceptance_criteria: A ship/kill decision recorded, backed by the signal log's empty-fire rate, not vibes.

title: Dogfood fixtures (the oracle) for the Probe trigger
description: should-trigger: C-suite 'it's a mandate'; 'one CSV good/one bad' false premise; 'mol-rapid symmetric with mol-feature' assumption. negative controls (must stay silent): 'why is bd grey', 'where is this CSV used'. Ref §3.
acceptance_criteria: Probe flags all 3 should-trigger fixtures AND stays silent (or one-shot-resolves) on both negative controls. This is the trigger-precision test — the skeleton's real success criterion.
close_reason: Dogfood fixtures recorded in the rule: should-trigger (C-suite mandate, one-CSV-good, mol-rapid-symmetry) + negative controls (why-is-bd-grey, where-is-CSV-used). They are the oracle for the observe phase.

title: Signal: record Probe firings to .gate-signal.jsonl
description: Wire _gate_signal.record() for each Probe firing: {fired, caught-real | empty-fire, why-excerpt, escape-used}. Ref §2. This is how 'helps vs annoys' is answered with data.
acceptance_criteria: Each Probe firing appends a record with the caught-vs-empty distinction; empty-fire rate is queryable from the log.
close_reason: Signal instruction baked into the rule (compliance-based, via _gate_signal.record gate=why-drilling decision=probe-fired|probe-empty|escape-used). Honest caveat recorded in the rule: rule-based signal relies on compliance (not a hook); the observe phase (7ki) tests whether empty-fire rate is low enough.

title: Depth-1 Probe as thin always-on rule + escape
description: Author claude/rules/why-drilling.md: trigger (load-bearing why that is thin/authority-shaped/user-asserted-unconfirmed) + one-question Probe + stop-rule (confirmed observable outcome OR flagged unconfirmed inference w/ named confirmer). Non-blocking by default. First-class one-word escape ('just answer'/'skip'). Ref: openspec/changes/why-drilling-engine/tasks.md §1.
acceptance_criteria: Rule exists, lean (trigger+pointer, not full engine). Probe is non-blocking in non-interactive runs. Escape suppresses the probe and is captured as a waiver.
close_reason: Wrote claude/rules/why-drilling.md (Depth-1 Probe: thin always-on trigger + one-question probe + confirmed-outcome/flagged-inference stop-rule + one-word escape). Deployed to ~/.claude/rules/ + registered in INSTALL.sh, matching every other rule's deploy pattern.

title: why-drilling engine (Probe/Drill/Grill) — unify scattered why-asking
description: One why-drilling engine exposed at three depths (Probe=1 question default-on; Drill=root-cause; Grill=full interview). Consolidates the absent premise-check, mol-rapid diagnose root-cause, and /brainstorm Section 5 interview under one engine. Design: openspec/changes/why-drilling-engine/design.md. Triggered by C-suite demo anecdote + this session's own false-premise failures.
design: Home = skill body + thin always-on rule (Depth-1 trigger). Walking skeleton tests the RISKIEST assumption (does ambient thin-why detection help vs annoy?), NOT 'the Probe works'. Engine extraction + Depth-2/3 are follow-ups gated on Depth-1 proving useful. Full design in openspec/changes/why-drilling-engine/.
acceptance_criteria: DONE (epic) when: Depth-1 Probe flags the 3 dogfood should-trigger fixtures and stays silent on the 2 negative controls, AND after 1-2 weeks real use the empty-fire rate is low enough that the user keeps the Probe enabled. NOT done when child tasks merely closed — verify against the design's Proof of Delivery. Kill condition: high empty-fire on lookups OR user disables -> do not ship Depth-1, keep only opt-in deeper tiers.

title: ScheduleWakeup does not satisfy continuation-harness Stop gate; stale fallbacks replay completed work
description: Observed 2026-05-29. Two related defects in the ScheduleWakeup <-> continuation-harness interaction: (1) The harness Stop gate (continuation-harness.md path 2) expects a wakeup entry in ~/.claude/harness/threads/{session_id}/scheduled.json, but calls to the ScheduleWakeup tool did NOT satisfy the gate — every wait-turn while legitimately blocked on background work still got 'no_completion_or_resumption_proof' and a Stop block. So path 2 (the documented resumption escape) is effectively non-functional via the ScheduleWakeup tool; only verify-pass (path 1) or user-release (path 3) actually release the gate. (2) Fallback wakeups fire on their timer regardless of whether the primary work already finished, re-injecting the same /loop prompt — so a late fallback replays an already-completed task list (observed: a 05:30 fallback fired at 09:05 asking to redo the entire fxh remediation, all already done). Fix: make ScheduleWakeup write the scheduled.json entry the harness Stop gate reads (or teach would_block_stop.py to recognize ScheduleWakeup's own store), AND have fallback wakeups no-op / self-cancel when their tracked work already completed.
acceptance_criteria: A ScheduleWakeup call satisfies the harness Stop gate's resumption path (no spurious no_completion blocks while a registered wakeup exists), AND a fallback wakeup whose underlying work already completed does not replay a finished task list.
close_reason: Done + merged in PR #6; re-closing after the main-sync reverted the status again (beads working-tree coupling, tracked as mol-dtt).

title: Background Workflow runs die silently at ~13-min host task timeout (no completion signal)
description: Observed 2026-05-29 during the fxh-remediation workflow (run wf_847dcdad-5cc): the background Workflow process terminated TWICE at ~13 minutes elapsed — once mid-fxh.7 (after editing 8 of 12 hooks, before closing the bead), once mid-fxh.12 (after writing the deliverable, before closing). No completion notification fired; the orchestrator only discovered the death via a fallback ScheduleWakeup + manual ps/file-activity inspection. Resume-from-runId worked (cached completed agents) but a third death would have recurred at the same timeout. Impact: long multi-agent workflows (heavy cross-cutting phases like a 12-hook rewrite) cannot complete in one background run and require manual death-detection + resume or manual finish. Investigate the host task-timeout for local_workflow background tasks, and either (a) raise/remove it for workflows, (b) emit a clear timeout/death notification so the parent is re-invoked instead of silently stranded, and/or (c) have the workflow runner checkpoint+auto-resume across the timeout boundary.
acceptance_criteria: A workflow whose total wall-clock exceeds the current ~13-min limit either completes, or the parent agent receives an explicit death/timeout notification (not silence) and can resume without manual ps/file-activity forensics.
close_reason: Done + merged in PR #6; re-closing after the main-sync reverted the status again (beads working-tree coupling, tracked as mol-dtt).

title: Close the gate-signal learning loop: nothing reads/aggregates .gate-signal.jsonl
description: Surfaced by the q90 completeness-critic demo (finding S3). Gates append to .beads/.gate-signal.jsonl (verified ~497KB, 1977 records, 15 gates) per gate-design Rule 2, but there is NO consumer: no aggregation, no recurrence analysis, no half-life report. Writing signal nobody reads is petrification wearing a learning-loop costume. Build the read side: a recurrence/half-life report over the signal log (which gates fire most, which waivers recur) to feed Operating Rule 1 review. Related: declarative-gate-manifests-idea memory. Source: docs/assessments/2026-05-29-completeness-critic-demo.md.
close_reason: Closed

title: spec-id referential integrity: re-check anchors after a discovery/spec edit (orphaned --spec-id)
description: Surfaced by the q90 completeness-critic demo (finding M1). work-breakdown bakes --spec-id=...specs/{cap}.md#{requirement} into beads tasks; spec_id_enforcement validates the anchor resolves AT CREATION, but nothing re-verifies after a later discovery/spec edit renames a requirement heading — silently orphaning every baked-in spec-id. Add a re-validation pass (preflight or a doctor check) that flags beads whose --spec-id anchor no longer resolves. Source: docs/assessments/2026-05-29-completeness-critic-demo.md.
close_reason: Built claude/bin/spec_id_preflight.py: re-validates that beads' baked-in --spec-id anchors still resolve to a real '### Requirement:' heading, reporting orphans and exiting non-zero when any exist. Behavioral tests in test_spec_id_integrity.py: positive control (resolving anchor not flagged), negative control (renamed-heading anchor flagged + drives non-zero exit), and a shortcut-rejecting test (file-exists != resolved). Oracle: pytest test_spec_id_integrity.py -> 10 passed, exit 0.

title: Epic coverage/parent-completion gate: block closing a parent whose named seams lack covering children
description: Mechanical form of the cake-ta5.1 lesson now documented in work-breakdown SKILL.md (Per-Epic Requirements) and outcome-ownership.md (Child-Closure Is Not Parent-Completion). The docs are authoring-time/agent-discipline defenses; this bead is the runtime gate. On 'bd close <epic>', warn or block if (a) the epic carries no own acceptance oracle, or (b) seams named in the epic description map to no closed child. Must satisfy gate-design Rules 1-3 (escape path, persistent signal, value-not-presence) and ship with a behavioral test — a naive prose-match gate would be mock bureaucracy. Open question: how to enumerate 'named seams' from free-text reliably (likely requires the epic to carry a structured scope-coverage manifest, e.g. a verify-block per fxh.10's convention).
